example: I have been trying to weasel my way out of being on the stage for weeks.
ja: 巧妙に抜け出す、うまく逃れる

example: I watched an old TED Talk that Bernay Brown did on vulnerability.
zh: 脆弱性，易受傷害的狀態

example: She is a shame researcher and I am a recovering bulimic.
I am a recovering bulimic, alcoholic, and drug user.
zh: 正在康復中的暴食症患者

example: Dr. Brown defined courage as telling the story of who you are with your whole heart.
Making the unknown known is what is important.
ja: 未知を既知にすること

example: When I was eight years old, I started to feel exposed and awkward.
I was pushed out of my house and into school, all oily and pudgy and conspicuous.
I was oily and pudgy and conspicuous.
Our superhero capes are what keep us from having to feel much at all.
zh: 超級英雄的披風，比喻保護自己的偽裝
ja: スーパーヒーローのマント（比喩的に自分を守るもの）

example: Perfectionism and overworking are some of our superhero capes.
Snarkiness and apathy are superhero capes.
Every good and bad thing is deflected off of them.
My capes of addiction and pretending kept me safe and hidden.
We start out as extremely sensitive truth tellers.
I was so exhausted and so numb that I never had to go back.
I walked into my guidance counselor's office and said, actually, I'm not fine.
ja: 進路指導カウンセラー

example: I was sent to a mental hospital.
In the mental hospital, there was no pretending.
In the mental hospital, the jig was up.
Her arms were just sliced up like pre-cut hands.
I binged and purged for the first time when I was 8.
ja: 過食と嘔吐を繰り返す

example: I found myself on the cold bathroom floor hung over.
I was holding a positive pregnancy test.
That day on the bathroom floor, I decided to show up.
I didn't know how to be a sober person.
What they don't tell you about peeling off your capes is that it gets worse before better.
zh: 脫下偽裝，放下保護自己的外衣
ja: マントを脱ぎ捨てる（比喩的に偽りを捨てる）

example: Getting sober is like recovering from frostbite.
At first, it just feels kind of tingly and uncomfortable.
ja: ピリピリする、チクチクする

example: Then those feelings start to feel like daggers.
I finally accepted that sensitive is just how I was made.
I write and I serve people to help create a world.
Tell the truth and forgive each other for being human.
Together we can do hard things.
I used to numb my feelings and hide.
Feelings are just guides to tell me what is the next right thing to do.
Loneliness leads us to connection with other people.
Jealousy guides us to what we're supposed to do next.
Being overwhelmed helps us to ask for help.
The prizes are peace and dignity and friendship.
The prizes are peace and dignity and friendship.
We just need awkward, oily, honest human beings.
Dear Glennon, it's braver to be Clark Kent than it is to be Superman. Carry on warrior.
zh: 繼續前進的戰士（鼓勵用語）
ja: 戦い続ける戦士（励ましの言葉）
